revision: Taxonomy lock v5.2 locked — 23 operators definitively closed (CONJ_NO_OP_24 proved); LSE corrected to 4n F16; two-layer accounting policy formalized
breakthrough: add_gen = 2n discovered via LEdiv+DEML construction: lediv(x, deml(y,1)) = ln(exp(x)/exp(-y)) = x+y for ALL real x,y
X20_finding: EPL(x,y) = exp(x*ln(y)) = y^x is one of the 16 F16 operators (ELMl in Sixteen_Operator_Census). Therefore pow(x,y) = x^y = EPL(y,x) for x>0 is a single 1-node F16 application — not 3n. The 3n construction was for general domain where x can be negative (ln(x) undefined). This resolves the F17d paradox in X20.

construction_detail.combined: LEDIV(x, DEML(y,1)) = ln(exp(x)/exp(-y)) = ln(exp(x)*exp(y)) = ln(exp(x+y)) = x+y

table.construction_general: UNDEFINED â€” ln(x) undefined for x <= 0 in real F16
table.domain_general: not defined for x <= 0; no real F16 tree computes ln for all reals

table.construction_general: ELSb(0,x) in the real-F16 sense requires x > 0. For x < 0: note that 1/x = -1/|x|; no single real-ELC 1-node tree covers all x != 0. However, the implementation (X8 audit) handles x != 0 including negatives â€” likely via the algebraic identity rather than a strict real-ln route.
table.domain_positive: x > 0 â€” ELSb(0,x) = exp(-ln(x)) = 1/x directly valid
table.domain_general: x != 0 â€” ELSb works in practice (X8 verified); strict real-ELC requires x > 0 for the ln step
table.notes: R16-C1; X8 audit confirms negative x also handled in implementation

table.construction_positive: ELSb(ln(x), y) = exp(ln(x) - ln(y)) = x/y, x > 0, y > 0
table.construction_general: elsb(exl(0,x), y): requires ln(x), so x > 0 is hard constraint; y < 0 works (X8 verified). General signed x cannot be handled in 1n. For x < 0: div(x,y) = neg(div(-x,y)) â€” adds neg step. Best general cost not fully resolved; 2n for positive x.
table.domain_positive: x > 0, y > 0 â€” single ELSb node if ln(x) is pre-computed; 2n total (ln(x) + ELSb)
table.domain_general: x > 0 required (hard, from ln); y != 0 (y < 0 works per X8). For x < 0 no clean single real-F16 tree.
table.notes: X7/X8 confirmed: x > 0 hard constraint; y can be negative
table.cost_note: cost_positive=1 counts ELSb alone assuming ln(x) pre-computed; full tree is 2n (EXL node + ELSb node)

table.construction_positive: EXL(0, DEML(x,1)) = ln(exp(-x)) = -x; or DEML(x,1)=exp(-x) then EXL gives -x
table.construction_general: EXL(0, DEML(x,1)) = ln(exp(-x)) = -x for all x; DEML(x,1)=exp(-x) > 0 always, so EXL is always valid
table.domain_positive: all x (DEML output is always positive, so EXL is always valid)
table.domain_general: all x â€” same construction works for all reals (neg is domain-universal at 2n)
table.notes: T09; 2n for all reals; positive domain gives no cost improvement

table.construction_positive: ELAd(EXL(0,x), y) = exp(ln(x)) * y = x*y, x > 0
table.construction_general: neg(mul(neg(x), y)): neg(x) costs 2n (all reals), mul_pos(neg(x),y) costs 2n (neg(x)>0 when x<0), outer neg costs 2n = 6n total. Handles all real x via negation wrapping.
table.domain_general: all real x, y via neg(mul(neg(x),y)) = 6n. Note: abs(x) outside real ELC but signed mul is achievable at 6n cost.
table.notes: T10u; F16 optimal for x > 0. X7/X8 confirmed domain restriction.

table.construction_general: LEdiv(x, EML(y,1)) = ln(e^x / e^y) = x - y for all x,y; EML(y,1)=exp(y) > 0 always, so LEdiv is always valid
table.domain_positive: all x, y (same as general â€” sub is domain-universal)
table.domain_general: all x, y â€” X8 verified 100/100 test points passed
table.notes: T33; 2n for all reals; X8 confirmed

table.construction_general: LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1)) = ln(e^x / e^{-y}) = ln(e^{x+y}) = x+y for all x,y; DEML(y,1)=exp(-y) > 0 always, so LEdiv is always valid
table.domain_positive: all x, y (same as general â€” add is domain-universal)
table.domain_general: all x, y â€” X8 verified 100/100 test points passed
table.notes: NEW v5 (ADD-T1): all real x,y. Replaced add_pos=3n and add_gen=11n with unified 2n.

table.construction_positive: EPL(0.5, x) = ELMl(0.5, x) = exp(0.5Â·ln(x)) = sqrt(x), x > 0 â€” single F16 node
table.construction_general: UNDEFINED â€” sqrt(x) undefined for x < 0 in real arithmetic; no real F16 tree covers all reals
table.domain_positive: x > 0 â€” natural domain for real sqrt
table.domain_general: not defined for x < 0 in real F16; complex sqrt is outside real ELC
table.notes: T_SQRT_1N; EPL(0.5,x) = ELMl direct primitive. Same mechanism as pow=1n. Corrected from v5.1 (was 2n). Verified: EPL(0.5,4)=2.0, EPL(0.5,9)=3.0, EPL(0.5,16)=4.0.

table.construction_positive: EPL(n,x) = ELMl(n,x) = exp(n * ln(x)) = x^n, x > 0 â€” single 1-node F16 operator from the 16-operator census
table.construction_general: exp(n * ln(x)): mul(n,ln(x)) = 2n (requires x > 0 for ln), then EML(result,1) = 1n more; total 3n. For general domain with possibly-negative x, ln is undefined â€” no clean real F16 tree; 3n is for positive-base case using expanded construction.
table.domain_positive: x > 0, n real â€” EPL(n,x) is a direct 1-node application of the ELMl operator from the F16 census
table.domain_general: x > 0 required in all real constructions (ln(x)); general negative x has no real F16 solution. 3n is the positive-base cost using expanded EXL/ELAd/EML tree rather than direct EPL.
table.notes: X20 RESOLUTION: EPL/ELMl = exp(x*ln(y)) = y^x IS in F16 census. pow(x,n)=x^n=EPL(n,x) for x>0 costs 1n (direct), not 3n. The 3n construction used EXL+ELAd+EML explicitly rather than recognizing EPL as a primitive. For positive domain: corrected to 1n.
table.cost_correction: positive domain corrected from 3n to 1n per X20 finding

total_positive.formula: exp(1) + ln(1) + recip(1) + div_single_elsb(1) + neg(2) + mul(2) + sub(2) + add(2) + sqrt(1) + pow_positive(1) = 14
total_positive.note: div counted as 1n via single ELSb assuming ln(x) precomputed (v5.3 reconciliation; equivalent breakdown uses div=2n full-tree with mul=1n via UpperBounds.lean — both sum to 14n). pow corrected to 1n from X20. sqrt corrected to 1n in v5.2 (EPL/ELMl direct primitive). v5.3 headline: 14n / 80.8% savings.

total_general.formula: exp(1) + ln(1) + recip(1) + div(2) + neg(2) + mul(6) + sub(2) + add(2) + sqrt(2) + pow(3) = 22
total_general.note: mul=6n via neg(mul(neg(x),y)): all 10 ops now have general-domain F16 trees.

totals.positive_headline: 14n / 80.8% savings (positive domain, exp=ln=mul=recip=pow=sqrt=1n via UpperBounds.lean; mul_pos reconciled + sqrt reconciled to 1n)
totals.general_headline: 22n / 69.9% savings (general domain, mul=6n via neg/mul/neg, pow=3n)

improvement_v5_to_v5_1: pow_positive drops from 3n to 1n (EPL/ELMl recognized as direct 1-node operator); positive-domain total drops from 18n to 16n; savings vs naive jumps from 75.3% to 78.1%
completeness: All 10 operations: positive domain <= 1-2 nodes each; general domain varies (mul has no F16 tree). Table is COMPLETE with dual-domain annotations.

open_problem_resolved.was: add_gen = 11n (best known); lower bound was open; conjecture was 11n tight (add_gen_11n_conjecture.json)
open_problem_resolved.now: add_gen = 2n PROVED by explicit construction. The 11n construction was not optimal.
open_problem_resolved.implication: Every polynomial-like sum of signed terms drops from 11n to 2n per addition.

X20_resolution.paradox: F17d in X20 asked: if ELMl is in the 16-operator census, why is pow = 3n?
X20_resolution.resolution: ELMl = EPL(x,y) = exp(x*ln(y)) = y^x IS in F16. pow(x,n) = x^n = EPL(n,x) for x>0 is 1 node. The 3n construction (EXL+ELAd+EML) was an expanded decomposition that failed to recognize EPL as a usable primitive. For positive domain: pow = 1n. The general-domain 3n figure remains the best known for the expanded-tree path; with EPL as primitive, even general positive-base pow is 1n.
X20_resolution.downstream_note: Any catalog equations containing pow(x,n) with positive base x have their positive-domain cost reduced by 2 nodes per pow occurrence.

linear_cost_law_update.note: The positive/general distinction collapses for add/sub/neg. mul and pow retain domain splits.

taxonomy_lock.conj_no_op_24: PROVED — no 24th operator passes PGC+AIT filter on open domain
taxonomy_lock.lse_correction: LSE corrected from 5n to 4n in F16 (direct: exp+exp+add+ln)
